title: Phase 2: Core Link Management
description:
Implement basic link CRUD and display functionality.

## Tasks
- Create Link model with fields: url, title, description, createdAt, clickCount, lastClickedAt, pinned
- Build link creation form with URL input
- Implement link list view with sorting (pinned first, then by frequency/recency)
- Add click tracking (increment count, update lastClickedAt)
- Implement link editing and deletion
- Add pinning/unpinning functionality

title: Phase 5: Credential Management
description:
Secure storage for OAuth tokens and API keys.

## Tasks
- Create encrypted credential storage (using node crypto)
- Build credential management UI
- Implement OAuth flows for:
  - Google (Docs, Sheets, Drive)
  - Slack
  - GitHub
  - Linear
  - Notion
- Add personal access token storage for services that support it
- Create credential refresh/rotation logic
- Build connection status indicators

title: Display click/usage counter on links
description:
Show usage statistics for each link in the list.

- Display click count
- Optionally show last clicked time (e.g., '3 clicks, last used 2h ago')
- Update counter when link is clicked

title: Phase 7: Polish & UX
description:
Refine the user experience and add quality-of-life features.

## Tasks
- Add keyboard shortcuts (quick search, navigation)
- Implement drag-and-drop reordering
- Add bulk operations (tag multiple, delete multiple)
- Create import/export functionality
- Add dark mode support
- Optimize for performance with many links
- Add browser extension for quick-add (stretch goal)

title: Active Tabs - Link Management App
description:
A local web app for managing, organizing, and intelligently summarizing browser links/tabs.

## Goals
- Replace browser tab hoarding with an organized link management system
- Smart link descriptions via content fetching and LLM summaries
- Track usage patterns to surface frequently-used links
- Support authenticated services (Slack, Google, GitHub, etc.)

## Key Features
1. Link storage with descriptions and tags
2. Click frequency tracking with smart ordering
3. Pin important links
4. Full-text search
5. Auto-generate descriptions from page content
6. LLM-powered summaries via Ollama
7. OAuth/token storage for authenticated services

title: Phase 4: Content Fetching & Auto-Description
description:
Fetch page content and extract titles/descriptions automatically.

## Tasks
- Create content fetcher service with URL parsing
- Implement title extraction from HTML (og:title, title tag, h1)
- Add description extraction (og:description, meta description)
- Build service-specific extractors:
  - Google Docs/Sheets (via title in page)
  - GitHub PRs/Issues (via API or page scraping)
  - Linear items
  - Notion pages
  - Slack messages (will need API)
- Create background job system for fetching
- Add manual refresh button for re-fetching content

title: Phase 1: Project Setup & Core Infrastructure
description:
Set up the foundational project structure and tooling.

## Tasks
- Initialize SvelteKit project with TypeScript
- Set up SQLite with Drizzle ORM
- Configure Tailwind CSS
- Create database schema for links, tags, and credentials
- Set up basic routing structure

title: Phase 6: LLM Summaries via Ollama
description:
Integrate local LLM for intelligent content summarization.

## Tasks
- Set up Ollama client integration
- Create summary generation service
- Build summary storage (cache summaries in DB)
- Add expandable summary UI component
- Implement summary regeneration
- Add model selection (allow different Ollama models)
- Create summary queue for batch processing
- Handle rate limiting and timeouts gracefully

title: Phase 3: Tags & Search
description:
Add categorization and search capabilities.

## Tasks
- Create Tag model and link-tag relationship
- Build tag management UI (create, edit, delete tags)
- Add tag assignment to links
- Implement full-text search across titles, descriptions, URLs, and tags
- Add tag filtering in the UI
- Build search results view

title: Add pin/unpin toggle button to link list
description:
Add a clickable pin button directly in the link list so users can quickly pin/unpin links without opening the edit page.

- Show pin icon (📌) that's clickable
- Toggle pinned state via form action
- Visual feedback for pinned vs unpinned state
